title: Fix false ASSIGNIN on interface input ports driven from outside
body:
## Summary

This patch fixes a false ASSIGNIN error when driving an interface input port from the instantiating scope via continuous assignment, which is legal per IEEE 1800-2023 Section 25.4. 

A gap found in CVV integration: https://github.com/openhwgroup/core-v-verif/pull/2724

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
interface my_if (input logic clk);
endinterface

module t;
  logic master_clk;
  my_if vif(.clk());
  assign vif.clk = master_clk;  // Legal but triggers ASSIGNIN
endmodule
```

On master:
```
%Error-ASSIGNIN: Assigning to input/const variable: 'clk'
```

## Changes

- `V3Width.cpp`: Added `m_ifaceVars` set, populated via `foreach<AstVar>` when visiting `AstIface` modules, to track variables declared inside interfaces
- `V3Width.cpp`: In the ASSIGNIN check, skip the warning when the target variable belongs to an interface and the current module context is not that interface (`!m_ifaceVars.count(varp) || VN_IS(m_modep, Iface)`)

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_interface_input_port_assign.{v,py}` -- drives interface input ports from instantiating scope (single port, multi-port, parameterized, consumer module)
- Added `test_regress/t/t_interface_input_port_assign_noinl.py` -- same test with `-fno-inline`
- Added `test_regress/t/t_interface_input_port_assign_bad.{v,py,out}` -- assigning to interface input from inside the interface still produces ASSIGNIN

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
